mnemonics: Don't Loop, Vectorize (DLV): For high-performance in pandas, avoid Python loops and use built-in vectorized operations instead.
keyPoints: Use vectorized operations with NumPy and pandas methods instead of iterating with loops
Select appropriate data types (e.g., category for strings, downcast integers) to reduce memory usage
Use efficient file formats like Parquet or Feather for faster I/O operations
commonMistakes: Applying functions row-by-row with .apply() instead of using vectorized alternatives
Using default object dtypes for strings instead of the more efficient 'category' dtype
Not using method chaining, which can create unnecessary intermediate copies of data
